title: Whole-grid lap snapshot encoder instead of row-local features
summary: Official F1 strategy explanations emphasize that pit calls depend on the bigger race picture, not just one car.
rationale: Official F1 strategy explanations emphasize that pit calls depend on the bigger race picture, not just one car. Set Transformer gives a permutation-invariant way to model all cars present on a lap and learn pit-wave, cover-off, and traffic pressure effects without hand-coding many rival features.
implementation_hint: For each `Race_Year x LapNumber`, build a set of car states using current lap features for all drivers. Feed the focal driver plus pooled set context into a small DeepSets/SetTransformer specialist, then use its OOF probabilities as an ensemble feature rather than replacing the tabular baseline.
expected_effect: Adds orthogonal signal around synchronized stop waves and local competitive pressure that single-row boosters only approximate with medians.
risk: Higher engineering cost and easier leakage if same-race snapshots appear in both train and validation folds.
